title: Upon The Death Of The Earl Of Dundee.[1]
content:
Oh, last and best of Scots! who didst maintain
    Thy country's freedom from a foreign reign;
    New people fill the land now thou art gone,
    New gods the temples, and new kings the throne.
    Scotland and thee did each in other live;
    Nor wouldst thou her, nor could she thee survive.
    Farewell! who dying didst support the state,
    And couldst not fall but with thy country's fate.
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, John Dryden delivers a powerful performance in "Upon The Death Of The Earl Of Dundee.[1]"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
